inline_comments.message: This translates into ccsdk-master-release-java-daily-checkstyle, and there is no such job... so what builds do you *really* want to trigger this CSIT? I noticed that this same mistake is done in the original ccsdk-csit.yaml and numerous other projects as well (I wonder where this copy&paste error originates from? I can't find any "*-daily-checkstyle" jobs anywhere from Jenkins?

These will overwrite the jobs defined above for healthcheck functionality if you insist to keep the project-name as "ccsdk'. If you're doing it like this, these should be
      - '{project-name}-oran-{stream}-verify-csit-{functionality}'
      - '{project-name}-oran-{stream}-csit-{functionality}'
Little things like these were what I meant when I said there might be some jjb synergies gained by having the project-name (and hence the corresponding csit/plans subdirectory) as 'ccsdk-oran' instead of 'ccsdk'

Right, that was bad advice as jjb reveals another non-obvious constraint: 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/ci-management-jjb-verify/4881/console

jenkins_jobs.errors.JenkinsJobsException: Failed to find suitable template named '{project-name}-oran-{stream}-verify-csit-{functionality}'

This narrows your choices somewhat. Either you add oran-polmansuite under the original ccsdk project as another functionality next to healthcheck (meaning that both you and Dan Timoney have to start receiving notifications from all of these) or you follow my original suggestion to name this 'ccsdk-oran' and adjust the csit/plans accordingly
Now you can reduce this to {project-name}-maven-docker-stage-master :D
